mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a supply-constrained regime reflects bottleneck pressures and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during supply bottlenecks yields positive 20-day SPX returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth signals expanding corporate revenue baselines, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 5-day SPX returns in >55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation accelerating beyond 4.0% forces central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 20-day SPX returns in >50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a liquidity-tight regime signals immediate consumer pullback, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS growth during tightening cycles fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).
